title: Vaccination Coverage Among Adolescents Aged 13√¢¬Ä¬ì17 Years √¢¬Ä¬î National Immunization Survey-Teen, United States, 2024 | MMWR
text:
¶ Local areas that received federal immunization funds under Section 317 of the Public Health Service Act were sampled separately. Those areas included Bexar County, Texas; Chicago, Illinois; Houston, Texas; New York, New York; and Philadelphia County, Pennsylvania. Two U.S. territories, Guam and Puerto Rico, were sampled separately in 2024.
** Persons living in all identified households with mobile telephones were eligible for interview. Sampling weights were adjusted for survey nonresponse, adolescent multiplicity (number of chances for selection), and noncoverage of the survey sampling frame, and were calibrated to known population totals. During 2011–2018, NIS-Teen sampled from a landline frame in addition to a mobile telephone frame; therefore, sampling weights were also adjusted for overlapping samples of mixed telephone users. A description of NIS-Teen single-frame survey methodology and its effect on reported vaccination estimates is available at National Immunization Survey-Teen, 2016-2017 | TeenVaxView | CDC .
†† The 2024 NIS-Teen sample included 7,707 adolescent girls and 8,618 adolescent boys. Adolescents from Guam (106) and Puerto Rico (234) were excluded from the national estimates.
§§ Estimates in this report include persons who might have received vaccinations on time or as catch-up.
¶¶ The Council of American Survey
